steps.title: Find the sentence subject
steps.content: The subject is "the 66-kilometer-per-second collision between these meteoroids and atmospheric gases." Its head noun is singular: "collision." Nearby plural nouns inside prepositional phrases do not control the verb.

steps.title: Reject plural verbs
steps.content: B, C, and D all use plural verb forms that would agree with a plural subject, not with the singular head noun "collision." The sentence needs the singular present-tense verb. The answer is A.
